name: Key Lime Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Mix crumbs, sugar, and butter; press into bottom of a 9-inch springform pan. Bake for 10 minutes.
3. Beat cream cheese, sugar, flour, juice, peel, and vanilla until well blended. Add the 3 whole eggs and egg white one at a time, mixing well after each addition. Pour over crust.
4. Bake for 40 minutes. Turn off oven; let cake cool as the oven cools down. Loosen cake from rim of pan; cool completely before removing rim.
5. Mix sugar and cornstarch in a saucepan. Gradually stir in water and juice. Bring to a boil over medium heat, stirring constantly. Boil 1 minute until clear and thickened, stirring constantly. Add small amount of hot mixture to slightly beaten egg yolk; return mixture to saucepan and cook for 3 minutes, stirring constantly.
6. Spoon topping over cheesecake and refrigerate at least 2 hours.
7. Garnish with paper-thin slices of lime.
I use regular lime peel and slices since I have no source for fresh key limes. If you do, all the better. I get the key lime juice from the King Arthur catalog.
